case_title: TAO WU CHEN VS. GAGANDEEP SINGH

docket_entries.description: STIPULATION TO USE JUDGE PRO TEM OR HEARING OFFICER. FILED BY CHEN, TAO WU SINGH, GAGANDEEP

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF OCT-25-2022 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF OCT-25-2022 BY JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID LAVINE. PLAINTIFF TAO WU CHEN IS PRESENT. DEFENDANT GAGANDEEP SINGH IS PRESENT. PARTIES WENT OUTSIDE THE COURT HALLWAY AT 10:41AM ATTEMPT TO WORK ON SETTLEMENT. PARTIES CAME BACK IN COURT AND STATED THEY WERE AT IMPASSE AT 11:00AM. NO EXHIBITS RETAINED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID LAVINE; CLERK: D. AU. NOT REPORTED. (D506)
MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $500.00 FILED BY CHEN, TAO WU AS TO DEFENDANT SINGH, GAGANDEEP ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR OCT-25-2022 AT 10:00 AM IN DEPT 506
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF OCT-25-2022 - JUDGMENT.
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF (SERVICE FEE $75) SERVED SEP-03-2022, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON NATURAL PERSON AS TO DEFENDANT SINGH, GAGANDEEP

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF CHEN, TAO WU IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT SINGH, GAGANDEEP UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, PLAINTIFF HAD THE OPPORTUNITY FOR INSPECTION OF THE MOTORCYCLE BY A MECHANIC PRIOR TO PURCHASE, BUT DID NOT AVAIL HIMSELF OF AN INSPECTION UNTIL AFTER THE SALE. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WITHHOLDING OF $500 BY DEFENDANT UPON RETURN OF THE MOTORCYCLE BY PLAINTIFF WAS REASONABLE GIVEN DEFENDANT'S ASSOCIATED EXPENSES. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS NO EXHIBITS FILED
